level_signals: 8 to 12 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM strategy roles
At least 3 years in a senior or director level leadership role
Will lead the Revenue Operations function while overseeing Deal Desk and Enablement
Comp range $180,000 - $250,000 consistent with Director-level at a high-growth startup

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8 to 12 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM strategy roles
At least 3 years in a senior or director level leadership role
Experience building Revenue Operations infrastructure in high growth SaaS, PaaS, or infrastructure companies
Deep CRM expertise with Salesforce preferred
Strong understanding of forecasting, revenue metrics, and GTM analytics
Experience supporting both PLG and enterprise sales motions
Experience designing compensation plans, quota frameworks, and territory models
Strong cross functional leadership and communication skills
Experience implementing operational structure in fast growing environments
Active use of AI tools for operational efficiency and forecasting workflows
Successful completion of a background check

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in cloud infrastructure, AI infrastructure, developer tools, or ML platforms
Experience with usage based pricing and consumption models
Experience implementing CRM, CPQ, or forecasting platforms
Experience supporting technical GTM organizations selling to enterprise buyers
Experience in high growth startup or scaleup environments

explicitly_excluded: Candidates requiring employment visa sponsorship (currently unable to sponsor)

scope.surface: Revenue Operations function including Deal Desk and Enablement, covering Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS/PaaS/infrastructure; PLG and enterprise sales motions; usage-based and consumption pricing implied
core_responsibilities: Own CRM architecture, data governance, and GTM systems across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success
Build scalable analytics frameworks covering lead generation, pipeline conversion, retention, and expansion
Maintain revenue reporting models supporting ARR, MRR, NRR, CAC, LTV, and forecasting metrics
Evaluate and implement revenue technology systems including CRM, CPQ, BI, and forecasting tools
Design and manage revenue forecasting processes across enterprise and PLG motions
Lead weekly pipeline reviews and establish CRM hygiene standards
Build dashboards and reporting infrastructure for executive and board level visibility
Partner with Finance and executive leadership on compensation planning and territory design
Build scenario planning models for quota capacity and headcount forecasting
Provide strategic oversight for Deal Desk and Enablement functions
Partner on CPQ implementation, onboarding infrastructure, and GTM playbook development
Serve as the operational bridge between Sales, Marketing, Finance, Legal, and Customer Success
Implement AI tools and workflow automation across Revenue Operations

green_flags: Equity (stock options) offered to all team members
Remote-first company with globally distributed team
Company at meaningful scale ($120M ARR) with clear growth trajectory
Role has broad ownership and strategic influence at executive/board level
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available, limiting candidate pool
Compensation range is wide ($70K spread), suggesting level may be finalized during interviews
Role spans Revenue Operations, Deal Desk, and Enablement simultaneously — potentially very broad scope for one leader
Company founded in 2022 — very early stage for a Director-level RevOps function, suggesting high ambiguity and build-from-scratch environment

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (Director / People Manager inferred from 'lead the Revenue Operations function while overseeing Deal Desk and Enablement' and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from PLG + enterprise + $120M ARR + infrastructure product description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed as KPIs in JD)
location.type (inferred as remote-us from 'remote-first, globally distributed company' and 'eligible to work in the United States' constraint)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'will be narrowed during the interview process based on...location')
